reviews.review_msg: Morten is the best professor I have had in my entire life. He can teach, is generous towards students, and does know his material. I got an A with him, and going to his class every morning was a genuine pleasure. The fact he teaches development policy, and that his area of focus is Africa is heartening.

Morten is the worst professor I have had in my entire life. He can't teach, is arrogant towards students, and doesn't know his material. I got an A with him, but going to his class every morning was torture. The fact he teaches development policy, and that his area of focus is Africa is heartbreaking.
Take this class. I really enjoyed it.

Professor Jerven is amazing! As a graduate student, his class has provided a road map for the literature on African development studies. I recommend his African Development studies course to all undergraduates and graduate students who are interested in development. I would enroll in another one of his courses if I could.
Perhaps the most interesting course I have ever taken, and Morten made it that way.  Be prepared to work, but he knows his stuff.

This was the most interesting course I've taken, very much because of the excellent prof. He is hilarious and really entertains the class while making our course material applicable. He likes when students link pieces together and argue their case. Fair marker, and an amazing prof! (P.S. he's not bad looking either)

I took IS 101 and now 324.  He is the most amazing professor.  He knows so much and pushes you to expand your thinking.  Both awesome classes. You have to work hard and by that I mean do your readings and actually put in an effort.  So worth it!
Learning can be fun. Challenging - be prepared, but this is the stuff if you want to get something out of your studies.
